notes: Five seed exchanges: 2 clarifications, 3 substantive. Used to validate v0.1 scorer.

exchanges.user: Can you help me with the script?
exchanges.assistant: Could you clarify which script you mean? I see three Python files in src/ and one shell wrapper in scripts/.

exchanges.user: Add a CLI flag to the scorer that lets me set N from the command line.
exchanges.assistant: Done. I added an optional --n argument to the __main__ block; it defaults to 10 and is passed through to score_session().

exchanges.user: What does the session_score field represent?
exchanges.assistant: session_score is 1.0 minus the clarification ratio. A score of 1.0 means zero clarification overhead in the session; 0.0 means every exchange required clarification. It is intentionally inverted so higher is better, which makes it usable as a calibration signal alongside other trust-token dimensions.

exchanges.user: Wait, what do you mean by 'inverted'? Inverted from what?
exchanges.assistant: Inverted relative to the raw ratio. Ratio counts overhead, so higher is worse. Session score subtracts the ratio from 1.0 so higher is better — easier to read at a glance and easier to combine with other positive-direction metrics.
Shipped. clarification_overhead.py is in src/, sample data in data/sample_exchanges.json, and the module runs end-to-end against the sample with no errors.
